description:
Rewrite docs/SPEC-federated-state.md around the unified tracker model (see parent bl-e118), and amend docs/SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md to stay consistent. SPEC/docs only -- no code; implementation is the sibling refactor balls.

Scope:

1. Rewrite the federated SPEC: retire the 'federated mode' framing entirely. One model -- the tracker address (state_url, state_branch), always present with an implicit default, materialized as a clone at .balls/state-repo universally. Standalone vs federated is just the address value. Document config ownership split (config.json repo-owned, project.json project-owned on the tracker branch, project wins on overlap), the retirement of the master.json pointer, plugin config as project config, and the tracker/workspace terminology. Rename the file to SPEC-tracker-state.md -- 'federated' is no longer a mode. Carry the design rationale from the review conversation into a 'Why' section. Honest, prescriptive conformance section: the current code is mid-migration; the tests gate the refactors.

2. Amend SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md #4: the tracker branch carries the project's shared state -- task files AND project config (project.json, plugins/) -- not 'tasks and nothing else'.

3. Amend SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md #5: de-hedge. Drop 'if we find dep churn, split them too' -- deps stay in the task file, decided, no sidecar. Reconcile requirement 5 with requirement 1: the invariant is one compact single-line value per top-level field (verified against src/task.rs serialization); disjoint-field edits never collide; same-field edits are a genuine conflict for the resolver; nested objects are permitted when the whole field is one self-contained line. No conditional/provisional language.
title: SPEC sweep: rewrite for the unified tracker model
